summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a 1977 second set that shines on Iko Iko, Wharf Rat and Sugar Magnolia despite missing tracks and occasional performance hiccups.
review: The recording (soundboard) sounds clear and vivid for most of the set, though listeners note drum dropouts and a few beat‑drops in early songs. Musically the night delivers classic ’77 energy with a funky Iko Iko groove, an inspired Wharf Rat jam and a soaring Sugar Magnolia, even if Jerry’s guitar/vox slips on Passenger, Terrapin and Ramble On Rose. Overall the show is praised for its high‑octane moments while being hampered by missing songs and some technical glitches.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is strong on several jams but marred by performance issues and missing material; recording quality is generally good with occasional dropouts, leading to a weighted rating around 3.7.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall mix with occasional drum drop‑outs and missing transition into Iko; some beat‑drops on early songs, but most of the set sounds crisp and full.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is incomplete (first two songs missing) but picks up momentum with a peppy Ramble On Rose, then moves through a rocky middle before delivering strong jams on Iko Iko, Wharf Rat and Sugar Magnolia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Innovative riff in Wharf Rat praised, but guitar tone and vocal delivery flagged as weak on Passenger, Terrapin and Ramble On Rose.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh highlighted for 'bouncing notes off the ceiling' early in the set, creating a rain‑like soundscape.
band_member_comments.Keys: Possible guest appearance by Ned Lagin speculated; no concrete keyboard commentary.
band_member_comments.Drums: Dropouts noted in drum track and transition into Iko; overall groove on Iko praised.
